title: Simultaneous EEG and fNIRS recordings for semantic decoding of imagined animals and tools
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous EEG and fNIRS recordings from 12 participants performing semantic imagery tasks involving silent naming and sensory-based imagination of animals and tools. Participants engaged in visual, auditory, and tactile imagery modalities while 64-channel EEG (2048 Hz sampling rate) and continuous-wave fNIRS data were acquired using integrated EEG-fNIRS montages. The dataset supports research in semantic brain-computer interfaces and multimodal neuroimaging analysis.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system with 64 electrodes positioned according to the 10-20 system, plus bilateral earlobe reference electrodes, galvanic skin response measurement, and respiration monitoring at 2048 Hz sampling rate. fNIRS data were collected using a NIRx NIRScoutXP continuous wave system with 8 light sources and 4 detectors in integrated fNIRS-EEG caps using frontal and temporal montages.
